notes: As part of internal evaluation trials, the Milan-2T ATGM with indigenous critical subsystems was successfully test fired at MHOW.
Trials for the upgraded Akash missile with an indigenous RF seeker were successfully conducted during the annual Ex-Astrashakti.
Flight trials of the Dhruvastra missile in a limited envelope from an ALH MK-IV helicopter were successfully conducted in desert and high altitude areas.
Successful firing of the Quick Reaction Surface to Air Missile against a UAV was demonstrated, destroying receding and crossing targets.
AKASH-NG guided missions were successfully flight tested at ITR Balasore to intercept a low flying target.
The Release Flight Trial (RFT) of the Air launched Long Range Glide Bomb Gaurav with a PCB warhead was successfully completed.
A flight trial of the Very Short Range Air Defence System (VSHORADS) was carried out successfully at Pokhran Field Firing Ranges.
Three Electro-Optical Smart Anti Airfield Weapons (EO-SAAW) with Cooled Seeker Mk1 were successfully flight tested to demonstrate seeker guidance.
The Air to Surface missile RudraM-II was successfully flight tested from a Su-30 Mk-I fighter aircraft off the coast of Odisha.
Ship and dynamic trials of the Extended Range Anti-Submarine Rocket (ERASR), including Range Table Validation, were evaluated successfully at Balasore.
The Phase III trial of the Submarine Fired Decoy (SFD) was successfully conducted.
Two full range missions of the Submarine Launched Cruise Missile (SLCM) were conducted successfully from an underwater platform.
The complete chain of the Vertical Launch Short Range Surface to Air Missile weapon system was validated during flight trials at Balasore.
The Indigenous Technology Cruise Missile (ITCM) powered by an indigenous Small Turbo Fan Engine was successfully launched from ITR Balasore/Chandipur.
The first flight of the Long Range Land Attack Cruise Missile (LRLACM-01) was successfully conducted from ITR Chandipur.
Instrumented Development Flight Trials of the Laser seeker based Anti-Tank Guided Missile were conducted successfully from the Arjun MBT.
Dynamic trials of the Electrical Heavy Weight Torpedo (EHWT) successfully demonstrated controllability, stability, and thermal battery firing.
Phase-I and Phase-II trials of the Medium Range Microwave Obscurant Chaff Rocket (MR-MOCR) were successfully carried out from INS platforms.
